metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the round-navigation URL contract (e.g., `GET /ticket/<id>?round=N`) in the plan so the executor can anchor the oracle and the server route to the same shape.
findings.finding: The round stepper (`◂ round n of N ▸`) is described as server-rendered with no JS, but the plan never specifies the interaction model — URL query parameter, form GET submission, or link-per-round — leaving a real design decision to the executor. The AC oracle says 'requesting the older round' without anchoring that to a URL shape, which means the executor cannot write a deterministic proving command (e.g., `GET /ticket/<id>?round=2`) without guessing the contract.
findings.location: Scope / Acceptance Criteria — round-selector AC (`◂ round n of N ▸` stepper)
findings.suggested_fix: Add one line to the Scope: 'The round stepper renders as `<a href="/ticket/<id>?round=N">` links; the server route reads `?round` (default: latest).' Update the AC oracle to read: 'asserting `GET /ticket/<id>?round=1` renders that round's findings.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the convergence visualization spec whether each decision type (Blocking / Advisory / Dropped / Indeterminate) gets its OWN polyline (4 total) or whether advisory/dropped/indeterminate share ONE muted polyline (2 total), and align the AC oracle count accordingly.
findings.finding: The convergence line-graph AC hard-codes 'exactly 4 `<polyline>` (one per decision series)' but the plan body describes the muted series as 'advisory/dropped/indeterminate' together without clarifying whether each is a separate polyline or all three share one muted polyline. If the implementation renders one combined muted series the AC fails (3 polylines: 1 blocking + 1 advisory + 1 muted-combined for dropped/indeterminate); if it renders four the plan body's grouping prose is misleading.
findings.location: What section — convergence visualization spec; Acceptance Criteria — convergence AC
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is 4 separate series, add to the spec: 'Each of the four decision types (Blocking, Advisory, Dropped, Indeterminate) is a distinct `<polyline>`; zero-count series are emitted as flat lines.' If the intent is 2 (blocking + muted-rest), change the AC to 'exactly 2 `<polyline>`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the AC #3 oracle to name the specific HTML landmarks or label strings to assert — e.g., 'asserting the expanded `<details>` contains elements with labels `Finding`, `Verification`, `Decision`, `Coaching` AND that the `Verification` sub-answers block and the `Decision` reason text are non-empty strings from the seeded fixture.'
findings.finding: AC #3 (finding row expansion) specifies the oracle as 'asserting the expanded finding HTML contains content from each of the four passes' without stating WHAT content to assert — a field name, a label string, or a structural element. 'Contains content' is a subjective criterion that an executor could satisfy by asserting any non-empty string, making the test trivially passable with a stub template.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #3 (finding row expansion oracle)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'contains content from each of the four passes' with: 'asserting the expanded `<details>` contains the headings/labels Finding, Verification, Decision, and Coaching (each present in the DOM), and that the Verification sub-answers block and Decision reason field are non-empty and match the fixture's seeded pass-2 sub-answers and pass-3 reason string.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the convergence line-chart polyline count: either (a) update the body to say all four decision-series slots are always emitted (even if empty/zero-length), or (b) update AC6's oracle to assert 'one `<polyline>` per non-empty decision series' and seed the test fixture with findings in all four series so the count is deterministically 4.
findings.finding: The plan body says the convergence line chart has 'one <polyline> per decision series' (variable count, depending on which decision series have data across rounds), but AC6 hardcodes the oracle assertion as 'exactly 4 <polyline> (one per decision series)' — this is a cross-section contradiction: if a 4-round ticket has findings in only 3 decision series, the body's rule yields 3 polylines but the AC demands 4.
findings.location: What section — convergence visualization spec ('one `<polyline>` per decision series'); AC6 oracle ('exactly 4 `<polyline>` (one per decision series)')
findings.suggested_fix: Seed the AC6 test fixture with findings in all four decision series (BLOCKING, advisory, dropped, indeterminate) across 4 rounds, so 'exactly 4 polylines' is correct for that specific fixture. Then update the body to clarify: 'one `<polyline>` per decision series present in the data; a test fixture covering all four series will produce exactly four.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion covering the Trust Signals deliverable: assert that a round header in the rendered HTML contains the gate/model version, timestamp, and (for a signed record) the certified badge element; for an unsigned record, assert the badge is absent.
findings.finding: The described 'Trust signals' deliverable — gate/model version, timestamp, and a 'certified' badge in each round header — has no covering acceptance criterion; it is a named, specified UI feature with observable behavior but no oracle test.
findings.location: What section — 'Trust signals in each round header'; Acceptance Criteria section (no AC maps to this deliverable)
findings.suggested_fix: AC (new): 'Each round header renders a trust-signal caption containing gate version, model version, and timestamp. When the record carries a signature, a "certified" badge element is present near the verdict; when unsigned, it is absent. Oracle: an E2E test seeding one signed and one unsigned round, asserting badge presence/absence and caption content.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add observable done-definitions for WCAG 1.4.3 (color contrast ≥ 4.5:1 for text, including verdict/decision chip labels) and WCAG 2.4.7 (visible focus indicator on all interactive elements including <details>/<summary> and round-stepper controls) to either the visual system spec or AC10.
findings.finding: The visual system section specifies color use and a type scale, and AC10 checks heading structure and <details> presence, but neither the plan body nor any AC specifies color contrast ratios meeting WCAG 1.4.3 (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text) or visible focus indicators meeting WCAG 2.4.7 — the two most commonly failed WCAG 2.1 AA criteria for a new page.
findings.location: Visual system section; Approach rationale (cites WCAG 1.4.10, 1.1.1 but not 1.4.3 or 2.4.7); AC10
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC10 to include: 'All interactive elements (details/summary, stepper controls, anchor chips) have a visible focus indicator (not suppressed by inline CSS). Verdict and decision chip text meets a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 against its background (WCAG 1.4.3).' Add a note to the visual system section pinning chip label contrast to ≥ 4.5:1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a durable documentation artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or design-doc) capturing the three novel decisions — finding-centric IA (vs pass-as-navigation), neutral threshold meter color inheritance (and why signed green/red was rejected), and per-finding block_threshold persistence (vs runtime re-derivation) — and reference it from the Scope section.
findings.finding: The plan introduces several novel architectural decisions — finding-centric IA, neutral threshold meter (decision-color inheritance replacing signed delta), convergence visualization switching rule (≤3 bars / ≥4 line), and the pattern of persisting per-finding `block_threshold` on the record rather than re-deriving it at render time — but the Scope section lists only code and template files with no mention of updating an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc to capture these decisions for future maintainers.
findings.location: Scope section; Approach rationale section
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/adr/NNN-audit-page-ia.md` covering: (1) finding-centric IA rationale; (2) neutral threshold meter and why decision-color inheritance was chosen over a signed delta; (3) per-finding `block_threshold` persistence contract and what it means for criteria_routing changes. Add this file to the Scope list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either cite the basis for the ≤3/≥4 boundary (UX research, pilot data) or surface it as a named constant (e.g., `CONV_BAR_MAX_ROUNDS = 3`) in the server/template so it can be adjusted without hunting for the magic number.
findings.finding: The convergence visualization switching threshold (≤3 rounds → bar chart, ≥4 rounds → line chart) is a display heuristic hardcoded into the template logic with no configuration point, and the plan gives no basis (measurement or UX research citation) for why 3 is the right boundary — making it a magic number that could need adjustment as real ticket data accumulates.
findings.location: What section — convergence visualization paragraph; AC #6
findings.suggested_fix: Define `CONV_BAR_MAX_ROUNDS = 3` in `server.py` (or a config module) and pass it to the template context, replacing the literal threshold in the template condition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a structured error-handling path in the `/ticket/<id>` route (catch read-layer exceptions, log with ticket id + error context, return a user-visible error panel rather than a raw 500) and note at least one observable signal (log line or metric counter) for route failures in the plan or AC.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new server route reading from the story-E read layer but specifies no error handling or observability instrumentation for that I/O path. Unexpected read-layer failures (connection error, schema mismatch, missing record) would surface as unhandled 500s with no structured log/metric, making silent failures invisible to operators.
findings.location: Scope — `src/rebar/audit/server.py`; Compatibility & rollback section
findings.suggested_fix: In `server.py`, wrap the read-layer call in a try/except that logs `{ticket_id, error_type, message}` at ERROR level and renders a named error partial (e.g. `error_panel.html`) so the failure is both user-visible and structurally observable; add an AC asserting the error panel is rendered when the read layer raises.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (with E2E oracle) specifying the server's behavior when `/ticket/<id>` is called with a nonexistent ticket ID (e.g., returns HTTP 404 and renders an explicit 'ticket not found' error page), covering the failure path for this new user-facing route.
findings.finding: The `/ticket/<id>` route is a new user-facing endpoint, but no AC covers the failure path when the ticket ID does not exist in the data store (404 / not-found). AC9 covers a valid ticket with absent gates ('not yet run' panel), but a navigated-to or bookmarked URL with a nonexistent ID has no specified behavior or oracle, leaving that path untested.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC9 (empty/partial states) and Scope — `src/rebar/audit/server.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A request to `/ticket/<unknown-id>` returns HTTP 404 and renders an explicit error page (not a blank or 500). Oracle: an E2E test seeding one ticket and requesting a different, nonexistent ID, asserting HTTP 404 and the presence of a user-facing error message in the rendered HTML.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `jinja2` is already declared or guaranteed transitively available under the `reviewbot` extra; if not, add it explicitly (e.g. `reviewbot = ["fastapi", "uvicorn[standard]", "boto3", "jinja2"]`) so the audit server's template rendering doesn't fail on a clean install.
findings.finding: The plan introduces Jinja2 templates as the rendering engine, but `jinja2` is not declared in any existing `pyproject.toml` extra (the `reviewbot` extra lists only `fastapi` and `uvicorn[standard]`). FastAPI's `Jinja2Templates` requires `jinja2` to be installed (Starlette treats it as optional); if `jinja2` is absent, every route that renders a template fails at runtime. This is a net-new undeclared dependency for a single call-site (the audit page), and the plan gives no confirmation that it is already available transitively or a plan to add it to the extra.
findings.location: Scope section — `src/rebar/audit/server.py` + `src/rebar/audit/templates/*.html`; `pyproject.toml` `reviewbot` extra
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"jinja2"` to the `reviewbot` extra in `pyproject.toml` and call it out in the Scope section so implementers know to declare it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Evaluate whether the dual-form convergence visualization (separate bar-chart and SVG-polyline rendering paths branching at the ≤3/≥4 round boundary) is justified for an internal, loopback-only audit page with a small audience, or whether a single visualization form (e.g., CSS bars only, or a plain per-round count table) would adequately serve both the spot-checker and gate-debugger without the added implementation and test complexity.
findings.finding: The convergence visualization is a bespoke, dual-form inline-SVG/CSS rendering abstraction (stacked-bar `<div class='conv-bars'>` for ≤3 rounds, hand-authored `<svg class='conv-line'>` with `<polyline>` elements for ≥4 rounds) with zero existing precedent in the codebase and exactly one planned consumer (the audit page). The two-form switch adds substantial implementation complexity — two entirely different rendering paths, stable DOM markers, WCAG companion tables — for an internal, loopback-only, read-only page whose primary users are a small set of auditors and gate-debuggers. No scale evidence, profiling result, or AC cites a requirement that makes this complexity necessary over a simpler single-form approach (e.g., a plain HTML table with per-round counts and a single CSS bar implementation).
findings.location: What section — 'convergence visualization' bullet; AC6

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that stories A/B are explicitly scoped to add `block_threshold` to the per-finding sidecar record (both plan-review `sidecar.py::_slim()` and code-review `sidecar.py::build_payload()`), and add a hard `depends_on` reference to those stories so the meter AC is not blocked by missing data.
findings.finding: `block_threshold` is NOT currently persisted per finding in either the plan-review or code-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecar, but the plan asserts 'the value Pass-3 actually used is persisted PER FINDING by stories A/B (the plan-/code-review v2 sidecars), so the server reads it directly off the finding record — it does NOT do a runtime `criteria_routing` lookup.' Reading `plan_review/sidecar.py::build_payload::_slim()` confirms the field list does not include `block_threshold`, and `pass3_decide()` returns `{decision, reason, validity, impact, priority, severity}` — also without `block_threshold`. The threshold meter AC directly depends on this persisted value being readable from finding records.
findings.location: What section / threshold meter description; AC line 4 (threshold meter oracle); `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` `_slim()` function; `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` `pass3_decide()` return dict
findings.suggested_fix: In stories A/B, add `'block_threshold': block_threshold` to both `plan_review/sidecar.py::_slim()` and the code-review sidecar's per-finding output. In `pass3_over_findings`, capture the resolved `block_threshold` and include it in `decided.append(...)`. Then add an explicit `depends_on` to those sidecar-v2 stories in this story's plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the stories A/B (plan-/code-review v2 sidecars) that persist `block_threshold` per-finding are listed as `depends_on`, OR explicitly specify that the server will perform a runtime `threshold_for(finding.criteria)` lookup (and update AC4's oracle accordingly).
findings.finding: The plan states the threshold meter reads each finding's persisted `block_threshold` 'directly off the finding record' (from the stories A/B sidecars), but neither the current `plan_review/sidecar.py::_slim()` nor `code_review/sidecar.py::build_payload()` persists `block_threshold` per finding. The `_slim()` function captures id, norm_id, criteria, decision, priority, etc. — but not `block_threshold`. If stories A/B (the 'plan-/code-review v2 sidecars' named in the plan) are the dependency that adds this field, they must be listed as `depends_on`, or the server will need a runtime `criteria_routing` lookup — exactly what the plan says it does NOT do.
findings.location: What section — threshold display; Scope — 'The threshold meter reads each finding's persisted `block_threshold`/`priority`/`decision`'; AC4 oracle
findings.suggested_fix: Add the sidecar-v2 stories to `depends_on`, or revise the 'What' and AC4 to state that the server resolves `block_threshold` at render time via `threshold_for(finding.criteria, routing_map, gate=…)` using the existing `criteria/model.py` API.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `jinja2` (or `Jinja2>=3`) to the appropriate `pyproject.toml` dependency group (e.g. a new `audit` extra, or the existing `reviewbot` extra if the audit server ships alongside it).
findings.finding: Jinja2 is not declared as a dependency in `pyproject.toml` under any group (core, agents, reviewbot, dev, or any other optional extra), yet the plan specifies 'Server-rendered Jinja2' templates as the implementation technology for the audit page.
findings.location: Scope section: 'New `src/rebar/audit/templates/*.html` (Jinja2)'; Approach rationale: 'Server-rendered Jinja2 keeps the page a pure function of the read-layer data'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"jinja2>=3"` to the `reviewbot` extra in `pyproject.toml` (since the audit server is gated behind the same flag/extra as the review-bot), or introduce a dedicated `audit` extra.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify that stories A/B (the plan-/code-review v2 sidecars) MUST add `block_threshold` to each persisted finding record, and define a fallback rendering behavior (e.g., omit the tick / show a '?' marker) for pre-A/B sidecar records where the field is absent — so the threshold meter mechanism is complete even during the rollout window.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that each finding's resolved `block_threshold` 'is persisted PER FINDING by stories A/B (the plan-/code-review v2 sidecars), so the server reads it directly off the finding record — it does NOT do a runtime `criteria_routing` lookup.' However, the actual plan-review `sidecar.py` `_slim()` function and the code-review `sidecar.py` `build_payload()` do not include `block_threshold` in the persisted finding record. The field set currently stored per-finding is: `{id, norm_id, location, criteria, cohort, tier, decision, severity, validity, impact, priority, reason, drop_reason, verification, finding, suggested_fix, checklist_item}` — no `block_threshold`. If stories A/B are the upstream work that must add this field, the plan leaves that as an implicit dependency: it never specifies that those stories MUST extend the sidecar schema, nor does it define a fallback rendering path for pre-A/B records whose `block_threshold` is absent. The AC for the threshold meter test seeds 'KNOWN, DIFFERENT per-finding `block_threshold` values on their records' — but those records can't exist under the current schema. This is a mechanism gap: the meter's tick will have no data source until the sidecar is extended.
findings.location: What section — 'Threshold display = a neutral meter PER FINDING' and AC line for threshold meter; Scope — 'The threshold meter reads each finding's persisted `block_threshold`/`priority`/`decision`'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope or What section, add: 'Stories A/B are a hard prerequisite: they must extend the sidecar `_slim()` / `build_payload()` per-finding record to include `block_threshold` (the resolved value from `threshold_for` at decision time). This story's threshold meter reads that persisted field; for records that predate A/B (field absent), render the tick at `DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD` (0.95) with a visual indicator (e.g., a dashed tick + tooltip "threshold not recorded").' Add this to the data-contract subsection and to the AC for the threshold meter.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the 'Finding is the atomic unit' section that the server must invert the verdict-level coaching map (build a `{finding_id: [coaching_note]}` index before rendering) and define the expected behavior for the zero-coaching and multi-coaching cases (e.g., 'no coaching' shown as a quiet absent state for blocking findings; multiple coaching notes rendered as a list).
findings.finding: The plan displays per-finding coaching (Pass-4) as 'Coaching (pass-4 move + note)' inside each finding's expanded provenance block. However, the coaching data structure in the sidecar is verdict-level and move-centric: `[{move_id, move_name, subject, finding_refs[]}]` — each coaching record maps FROM a move TO a list of finding ids. To render coaching inside a finding row, the server must INVERT this map (collect all coaching notes whose `finding_refs` contains the current finding's id). The plan does not acknowledge this inversion step, nor does it address the edge cases it introduces: a finding referenced by zero coaching notes (no coaching to show), a finding referenced by multiple coaching moves (multiple coaching blocks?), or the join key alignment (the finding id format in `finding_refs` vs. the finding `id` field in the sidecar's per-finding records).
findings.location: What section — 'Finding is the atomic unit' paragraph; specifically 'Coaching (pass-4 move + note)' in the four-pass provenance description

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare `jinja2` as an explicit dependency in the new `audit` optional-dependency group in `pyproject.toml` (e.g. `audit = ["fastapi", "uvicorn[standard]", "jinja2>=3"]`) — Jinja2 is not bundled with FastAPI and is not currently present in any declared extra.
findings.finding: Jinja2 is named as the templating engine throughout the plan but is not declared in any `pyproject.toml` optional-dependency group, not present in the installed environment (UNRESOLVED), and not currently used anywhere in `src/rebar/`. FastAPI's `Jinja2Templates` (the natural integration point) requires `jinja2` as a separate pip package — omitting it from the extra's dependency list causes an ImportError at runtime when the audit module loads.
findings.location: Scope section: 'New src/rebar/audit/templates/*.html (Jinja2) — page + … partials'; Approach rationale: 'Server-rendered Jinja2 keeps the page a pure function of the read-layer data'; pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies]
findings.suggested_fix: Add a new `[project.optional-dependencies]` entry: `audit = ["fastapi", "uvicorn[standard]", "jinja2>=3"]`. If the audit server reuses the `reviewbot` extra's FastAPI instance, add `"jinja2>=3"` to `reviewbot` instead and call it out in the plan's Scope. Either way, the Scope section should name the extra and its deps explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the sidecar schema change (adding `block_threshold` to `_slim()`'s persisted per-finding fields) that stories A/B must deliver, so the audit server can read `finding['block_threshold']` directly off the record rather than re-deriving it at runtime.
findings.finding: `block_threshold` is not persisted on finding records in the current sidecar schema, yet the plan asserts the server reads it 'directly off the finding record' — no schema change is specified.
findings.location: What section — 'Threshold display = a neutral meter PER FINDING'; AC line 4 ('the persisted per-finding value, not a round-level scalar or a runtime criteria_routing lookup'); Scope — 'reads each finding's persisted block_threshold/priority/decision'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"block_threshold": threshold_for(f.get("criteria", []))[0]` (or the resolved value from pass3_over_findings) to `_slim()` in `sidecar.py`. The Data contract subsection of the parent epic should also enumerate this field explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the join rule for the Coaching section of per-finding provenance (e.g. 'filter verdict.coaching[] where finding.id in note.finding_refs, take the first match') and define the fallback rendering when a finding has no coaching entry.
findings.finding: The per-finding 'Coaching (pass-4 move + note)' provenance display has no specified join rule — coaching is stored at verdict level as `{move_id, finding_refs}`, not on each finding, and there is no fallback for findings with no coaching entry.
findings.location: What section — 'The finding is the atomic unit' subsection; finding provenance narrative: 'Finding → Verification → Decision → Coaching (pass-4 move + note)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'decision series' explicitly in the convergence viz spec as 'one polyline per distinct decision value from the vocabulary {block, advisory, dropped, indeterminate}' so the series count and identity are unambiguous.
findings.finding: The convergence viz's 'one `<polyline>` per decision series' leaves the series vocabulary undefined; the AC oracle's 'exactly 4 `<polyline>`' implies series = {block, advisory, dropped, indeterminate} but the plan never makes this mapping explicit.
findings.location: What section — convergence visualization spec (≥4 rounds branch); AC line 6 convergence oracle ('exactly 4 <polyline> (one per decision series)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ≥4-round convergence spec: 'Each decision series = one polyline per decision value in {block, advisory, dropped, indeterminate} (the four values pass3_decide emits), drawn as per-round counts on the Y axis.'

findings.finding: The convergence visualization AC specifies 'asserting the correct form' without pinning what structural HTML/SVG property distinguishes the ≤3-round bar form from the ≥4-round line-graph form. An executor writing the test has no concrete attribute, element type, or CSS class to assert on, so the oracle could be satisfied by a no-op or by asserting only the data table, leaving the visual-form branch untested.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — convergence visualization AC: 'asserting the correct form and the hidden table contents'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `data-viz-form` attribute (e.g., `data-viz-form="bar"` / `data-viz-form="line"`) to the visualization container element in the template spec, and update the AC oracle to: 'assert the container element has `data-viz-form="bar"` and contains at least one `<rect>` for the 2-round test; assert `data-viz-form="line"` and at least one `<polyline>` or `<path>` for the 4-round test, in addition to asserting the hidden table.'

findings.finding: The plan simultaneously requires 'no JS' (native <details>/<summary> only) and specifies 'a collapse all findings control per round.' These two constraints are mechanically incompatible: there is no browser-native way to imperatively collapse multiple open <details> elements without JavaScript. The control either must be JS-only (contradicting the no-JS constraint) or removed from the spec.
findings.location: What section — 'a collapse all findings control per round' bullet; Approach rationale — 'Native <details>/<summary> gives no-JS collapsibles'
findings.suggested_fix: Drop the 'collapse all findings' control from the What section (individual <details> toggle is sufficient for the auditor use case without a bulk-close) and remove the bullet so the no-JS constraint holds cleanly.

findings.finding: The plan simultaneously mandates 'no JS' (native `<details>` with 'no external JS/asset') and a 'collapse all findings control per round.' Collapsing multiple independent `<details>` elements simultaneously requires JavaScript — there is no pure-HTML mechanism to imperatively set the `open` attribute on a set of elements. This is an unresolved design contradiction: either the 'collapse all' control must be dropped, implemented with a small JS snippet (violating the stated constraint), or worked around with a fragile form/anchor hack.
findings.location: What section — 'a collapse all findings control per round' and Approach rationale — 'no-JS collapsibles with built-in a11y'; Scope — 'inline CSS; the convergence visualization + threshold meter as inline SVG/CSS (no external JS/asset)'
findings.suggested_fix: Drop the 'collapse all findings' control (it has no corresponding AC oracle test) to preserve the no-JS constraint cleanly; if it is desired, add an explicit note that it requires a small inline event-listener script and exclude it from the 'no external JS/asset' constraint.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
